response.justification: The candidate is a strong hands-on senior/staff engineer with roughly 19 years of broad experience spanning web, embedded, and low-latency systems, and he technically clears the 15-year minimum with a Bachelor's degree. However, this is a director-level AI strategy role requiring recognized AI expertise, demonstrated AI products or platforms, and strategic leadership influencing engineering and product direction — none of which is evidenced here. His AI exposure is limited to recent MCP/agent tooling contributions at Stripe, his career trajectory is product-team IC work rather than organization-level technical strategy, and the resume itself contains a credibility issue (a 'Founder, Datadog' entry describing an unrelated open-source GIS project).
strengths: 19+ years of broad engineering experience across web, mobile, embedded, and high-throughput low-latency systems
Recent hands-on exposure to AI-agent infrastructure (MCP code generators, agentic workflow tooling) at Stripe
Entrepreneurial and community track record: founded an open-source GIS platform, ran workshops, conference talks
concerns: No recognized AI/ML expertise or demonstrated AI products/platforms, the core preferred qualification for this role
Career has been senior/staff-level product engineering, with no evidence of director-level scope, technical strategy, or advising strategic customers
Resume inconsistency ('Founder, Datadog' describing an unrelated Geo-Data platform) raises accuracy concerns

key_factors.factor: Lack of recognized AI expertise and AI product track record
Seniority gap vs. director-level strategy scope
Meets 15-year experience minimum with broad, deep engineering background
